title: Delayed assignment do not initially update when using `--x-initial-value unique` and `+verilator+rand+reset+1`
code_blocks:
`timescale 1ns/1ps

module test;

reg a;
wire b;
initial a = 0;
assign #1 b = a;

always @(a, b) begin
	$display("%d a %b, b %b", $time, a, b);
end

initial begin
	$dumpfile("delay_dont_update.fst");
	$dumpvars(0, test);
	#10 a = 1'b1;
	#10 a = 1'b0;
	#10 $finish;
end

endmodule
../verilator/bin/verilator delay_dont_update.sv --binary --x-initial unique --debug --runtime-debug --timing --trace-fst &&  obj_dir/Vdelay_dont_update +verilator+rand+reset+1
                   0 a 0, b 1
                  10 a 1, b 1
                  20 a 0, b 1
                  21 a 0, b 0
                   0 a 0, b 1
                   1 a 0, b 0
                  10 a 1, b 0
                  11 a 1, b 1
                  20 a 0, b 1
                  21 a 0, b 0

Where `__VassignWtmp_h3df4871e__0` and `vlSelfRef.test__DOT__b`  are initialized to:


So: 
- `__VassignWtmp_h3df4871e__0` is initially `0` but `test__DOT__b` is `1`, they are out of sync.
- `test__DOT__a` is later set to `0` by the initial block,
- so the code does not enter the `if ((IData)(vlSelfRef.test__DOT__a) != (IData)(vlSelfRef.__VassignWtmp_h3df4871e__0))`, 
- so `Vdelay_dont_update___024root___act_comb__TOP__0____Vfork_1__0` don't run and `test__DOT__b` is not assignment the value of `__VassignWtmp_h3df4871e__0`, so they continue out of sync.

On the prior commit both values use the same initialization logic, which works with `+verilator+rand+reset+1`, but I guess it didn't work with randomize initialization, and that is why #6611 exists? I come to this conclusion while writing this paragraph, so I didn't investigate if that is the case.

I believe the proper solution is to somehow always initialize `__VassignWtmp_h3df4871e__0` with  the value of `test__DOT__b`.
